contraindications.clinicalRationale: Nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonists are suggested only for patients with eGFR ≥25 ml/min per 1.73 m2. Lower eGFR may increase risk of hyperkalemia and reduced drug efficacy.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: We suggest a nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist with proven kidney or cardiovascular benefit for patients with T2D, an eGFR ≥25 ml/min per 1.73 m2, normal serum potassium concentration

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: MRAs block aldosterone action and can cause hyperkalemia. Normal serum potassium concentration is required for initiation. Historical reports show higher incidence of hyperkalemia after publication of aldosterone antagonist trials.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: We suggest a nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist with proven kidney or cardiovascular benefit for patients with T2D, an eGFR ≥25 ml/min per 1.73 m2, normal serum potassium concentration

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

contraindications.clinicalRationale: Finerenone trials required serum potassium <4.8 mmol/l at screening for enrollment. Higher potassium levels increase risk of dangerous hyperkalemia with MRA therapy.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Serum potassium was required to be <4.8 mmol/l at screening for enrollment in FIDELIO-DKD

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

conditionDescriptions: Serum potassium >5.0 mmol/l for FDA-approved initiation
contraindications.clinicalRationale: FDA-approved initiation threshold requires serum potassium <5.0 mmol/l. Higher levels pose significant hyperkalemia risk with finerenone initiation.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: FDA approved initiation threshold: serum potassium <5.0 mmol/l

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Serum potassium monitoring required due to risk of hyperkalemia with MRA therapy
warnings.actionRequired: Monitor serum potassium at 1 month after initiation and then every 4 months. Hold therapy if potassium >5.5 mmol/l and restart when ≤5.0 mmol/l

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Use only in patients with type 2 diabetes and persistent albuminuria ≥30 mg/g despite maximum tolerated RAS inhibitor therapy
warnings.affectedPopulation: Patients with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease
warnings.actionRequired: Ensure patient has T2D, albuminuria ≥30 mg/g (≥3 mg/mmol), and is on maximum tolerated RAS inhibitor before initiating MRA

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease

warnings.warningText: Dose adjustment required based on eGFR level at initiation
warnings.actionRequired: Initiate 10 mg daily if eGFR 25–59 ml/min per 1.73 m2; initiate 20 mg daily if eGFR ≥60 ml/min per 1.73 m2

governance.sourceDocument: KDIGO 2022 Clinical Practice Guideline for Diabetes Management in Chronic Kidney Disease
